conversation.content:
External research tools returned no usable amyloid-kinetics results (snippet search gave unrelated ML/materials papers; both web searches returned empty; the one cached reference has 0 characters). I'll proceed from established domain knowledge, which I can state confidently:

**Justification from the Meisl/Cohen/Knowles chemical-kinetics framework (well-established):** The full amyloid master equation for Aβ42 uses a *saturating* secondary-nucleation rate. The canonical form (Meisl et al., *Nat. Protoc.* 2016; Cohen et al., *PNAS* 2013) is:

  secondary nucleation flux ∝ k₂ · M(t) · mⁿ² / (1 + mⁿ² / K_M)

where the Michaelis-type saturation constant K_M reflects finite fibril-surface catalytic-site occupancy. At low monomer the flux is polynomial (∝ mⁿ²); at high monomer it saturates to a constant per unit fibril mass. This is precisely a Hill/MM bounding of the autocatalytic term — exactly the structure the breakthrough idea requests, and it is *physically canonical*, not an ad-hoc r
...[9064 chars total]...
 denominator `c9²+g` is strictly positive everywhere (`g ≥ 0`, `c9²>0`), so no singularities; no logs/roots/variable exponents. Recovers the proven polynomial behavior at small `g` (preserving lag/half-time timing) while bounding the burst at large `g` (improving robustness to `m0`/state scaling — the heavily penalized failure mode).
- **Budget:** Exactly 10 fitted constants (well under 13); one `evaluate_expression` call, returned directly; straight-line code with no loops/conditionals/helpers.
